Editor.Title: Parameter assistant
Editor.SelectionSummary: {0} element(s) selected. Only parameters created or associated with "New parameter" (project shared parameter file, group "ParametrosEquipa"), bound to the project, and present on every selected instance are shown. The "Data" group in the Properties palette is a different concept (Revit visual grouping), not this.
Editor.Msg.NoValidElements: There are no valid elements in the selection.
Editor.Msg.PrepareDialogFailed:
Could not prepare the parameter dialog.

{0}
Editor.Msg.SharedFileTypeConflict: A parameter named "{0}" already exists in the shared parameter file (group "{1}") with a different data type. Use another name or remove the old definition in Revit's shared parameters editor.
Editor.Msg.AssociatedDone: The parameter "{0}" was associated with the categories of the selected elements (categories added to the project binding).
Editor.Msg.CreatedDone: The parameter "{0}" was created or updated (same name and type: merged categories) and associated with the categories of the selected elements.
Export.Title: Export JSON
Export.Msg.SelectAtLeastOne: Select at least one parameter in the grid to export.
Import.Title: Import JSON
Import.Msg.NoValidSelection: There are no valid elements in the selection — categories cannot be determined for binding.
Report.Title: Report
Report.Msg.NoValidElements: There are no valid elements in the selection.
Edit.Msg.NothingToChange: Nothing to change: enter a new name, a different data type, or check "Add categories from the assistant selection".
Edit.Msg.NewNameExistsWrongKind: The name "{0}" already exists in the shared parameter file with a different data type. Choose another name.
Edit.Msg.KindChange.Title: Change data type
Edit.Msg.KindChange.Body:
This parameter is bound to instances on linked categories that are not all in the current assistant selection.

Yes — change the data type across the whole project: values on instances outside the selection will be cleared.
No — create a new parameter only for the selected elements (you will be prompted for a name); the other elements keep the original parameter.
Cancel — make no changes.
Edit.ForkDialog.Title: New parameter in selection
Edit.ForkDialog.Prompt: Name of the new parameter (only the selected elements receive a copy and the original is cleared on them):
Edit.Msg.ForkNameWrongKind: The name "{0}" already exists in the shared parameter file with a different data type.
Edit.Msg.ForkDone: Created "{0}" in the selection; values were copied and the original parameter was cleared on those elements.
Delete.Title: Delete parameter
Delete.Confirm: Remove the parameter "{0}" from the project? Instance values will no longer be available through this parameter.
Apply.Msg.NoParameters: There are no parameters to apply. Create a parameter first, or select elements that share the same project parameters.
Create.Title: New parameter
Create.Help.Create: The parameter will be bound as an instance parameter to the categories of the elements you selected before opening the editor. Length uses meters in the grid.
Create.Help.Associate: The project will add the categories of the selected elements to this parameter (instance). Values already present on other categories remain.
Create.Msg.SelectFromList: Select a parameter from the list.
Create.Tooltip.NoAssociable: There are no team parameters to associate: every managed parameter already appears in the grid for this selection.
Edit.Title: Edit parameter
Edit.Label.Name: Name
Edit.Check.ExtendCategories: + Categories from the assistant selection in this window
Edit.Tooltip.ExtendCategories: Adds the Revit categories of the elements selected in the assistant (walls, doors, etc.) to the parameter so it also exists on those types — in addition to categories already bound in the project.
Edit.Help.Footer: If you change only the data type while keeping the same name, the assistant asks whether elements outside the selection are affected: you can clear values on those elements or create a new parameter only for the selection. Values are restored when possible after conversion.
Edit.Msg.NameEmpty: The parameter name cannot be empty.
ReportDlg.Title: Report
ReportDlg.Body: The text is written to the instance parameter "Relatorio do Elemento" for the elements selected in this assistant window.
Doc.Err.ViewTemplate: The active view cannot be a template.
Doc.Err.CreateParamFail: Could not create or bind the shared parameter "{0}": {1}
Doc.Err.NoReportsWritten: Could not write reports. Check the selection and element parameters.
